_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-16R Calcium Chloride (MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-16R: Calcium Chloride — MDHHS Reviewed 2025
pharmacological_category: Electrolyte — Calcium Supplement / Cardiac Membrane Stabilizer
indications: Calcium channel blocker (CCB) overdose — cardiac membrane stabilization
Cardiac arrest with suspected hyperkalemia (dialysis patient, known hyperkalemia)
contraindications: Concurrent digitalis (digoxin) toxicity — calcium may worsen digoxin toxicity
precautions: Calcium chloride is caustic — vesicant; extravasation causes significant tissue injury; confirm IV patency before administration
Administer slowly to avoid bradycardia and cardiac arrest
NOT compatible with sodium bicarbonate — will precipitate if given in same line; flush line between agents
Caution in patients on digoxin — calcium may precipitate ventricular dysrhythmia in digoxin toxicity
expected_effects: Stabilizes cardiac cell membranes against hyperkalemia-induced toxicity
Reverses hemodynamic effects of CCB overdose by restoring intracellular calcium
Protective effect in cardiac arrest secondary to hyperkalemia
side_effects: Bradycardia or cardiac arrest if given too rapidly
Tissue necrosis if IV extravasation occurs
notes: Calcium chloride (10% solution): 1 gram = 10 mL; contains approximately 3× more elemental calcium than calcium gluconate
Crush injury/CCB overdose: 1 gram slow IVP over 5 minutes
Arrest (dialysis/known hyperkalemia): 1 gram = 1 g / 10 mL IV/IO
NOT compatible with sodium bicarbonate — precipitates; flush IV line between agents
Digoxin patients: avoid calcium — consult online MC

dosing.population: Adult (>14 yr) — crush injury / suspected hyperkalemia
dosing.dose: 1 gram (10 mL of 10% solution) slow IVP over 5 minutes
dosing.notes: Verify IV patency; administer slowly; monitor ECG and BP
Cardiac membrane stabilization; may repeat per online MC guidance

dosing.population: Adult (>14 yr) — cardiac arrest (dialysis / known hyperkalemia)
dosing.notes: Give during arrest resuscitation when hyperkalemia suspected
Same cautions as adult; verify IV patency; administer slowly
